spec_match.rationale: Uses await using around a real IndexedDB readwrite transaction so commit and abort affect persisted object-store balances.

interactive.rationale: Run success / error scenarios; live log shows commit vs rollback vs release.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises 3 lines + 1 disposer for full transaction safety; delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers success commit and failure abort on the real IndexedDB store, including rollback of a queued debit.

summary: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp 1.1.1 on Chrome Canary: clicked run commit scenario and saw IndexedDB balances change to A=400/B=150 after tx.commit(); clicked run throw scenario and saw tx.abort(), transaction abort, original business-rule error, and balances remain A=500/B=50. Console was clean.
